pageDescription: Calculate earned run average (ERA) for pitchers

placeholders.inningsPitched: Enter innings pitched (e.g., 6.1 for 6 innings and 1 out)

definition.title: Earned Run Average
definition.description: The average number of earned runs a pitcher allows per nine innings pitched

interpretation.title: Performance Interpretation
interpretation.description: ERA under 3.00 is excellent, 3.00-4.00 is above average, 4.00-5.00 is average, and above 5.00 is below average

criticalWarningsList.notDiagnostic: This calculator provides ERA calculations only
criticalWarningsList.actual: Actual performance evaluation should consider additional statistics and context

importantNotesList: ERA only counts earned runs, not runs resulting from defensive errors
A lower ERA indicates better pitching performance
Park factors and opponent strength should also be considered
Sample size matters - ERA is more meaningful over many innings

messages.enterInformation: Enter earned runs and innings pitched to calculate ERA
messages.resultMessage: Your ERA calculation results are shown below

disclaimer.title: Important Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: This calculator provides ERA calculations based on standard baseball statistics formulas. Complete performance evaluation should consider additional metrics, context, and sample size factors not included in these calculations.

seekHelp.title: Baseball Resources
seekHelp.content: For comprehensive baseball statistics analysis and player evaluation, consult baseball reference sites, sports analytics resources, or professional scouts and analysts.

errors.earnedRuns: Please enter a valid number of earned runs
errors.inningsPitched: Please enter a valid number of innings pitched
errors.gamesPlayed: Please enter a valid number of games played

tooltips.earnedRuns: Number of earned runs allowed by the pitcher (runs not resulting from defensive errors)
tooltips.inningsPitched: Total innings pitched, including partial innings (e.g., 6.1 for 6 innings and 1 out)
tooltips.gamesPlayed: Number of games in which the pitcher participated (optional for additional context)

enter_pitching_statistics_to_calculate_e: Enter pitching statistics to calculate Earned Run Average
era_earned_runs_total_innings_game_innin: ERA = (Earned Runs ÷ Total Innings) × Game Innings
total_innings_whole_innings_outs_3_each_: Total Innings = Whole Innings + (Outs ÷ 3). Each out represents 1/3 of an inning.
earned_run_average_era_measures_how_many:
Earned Run Average (ERA) measures how many earned runs a pitcher allows per nine innings
                        pitched. It's one of the most important statistics for evaluating pitcher performance.
